races.name: Volta Ciclista a Catalunya – Stage 1
races.description: The 105th Volta a Catalunya opens with a deceptively punchy circuit around the Costa Brava — the race's fifth consecutive Grand Départ in Sant Feliu — featuring a category 1 mid-race climb and a notoriously twisting, technical final 23km that ramps to ~5% in the last 500m, making positioning and an explosive finish crucial for GC men and opportunists alike.

broadcasts.notes: Stages 1–3 stream on Discovery+; from Stage 4 (March 26) coverage moves to HBO Max. TNT Sports TV channels remain the same throughout. Discovery+ subscription ~£30.99/month; new Saver Plan on HBO Max from £25.99/month (12-month term).
Volta a Catalunya is an ASO race, exclusive to Peacock in the US. Premium plan $10.99/month or $109.99/year. Premium Plus (no ads) $16.99/month.
Free with English commentary on SBS On Demand.
Free-to-air on Spanish national public broadcaster. All stages live.
Free on Catalan public TV via Esport3 and 3Cat streaming.
Eurosport carries coverage across 50+ European countries. Stream via Discovery+ or HBO Max depending on country.
TV2 subscription required in Denmark and Norway.
Claro Sports subscription. Caracol/RCN for Colombia specifically.

data_confidence.notes: Start time (12:50 CET / 11:50 GMT) and finish (~17:00 CET) confirmed by official Volta Catalunya website and multiple preview sources. Distance 172.7km confirmed by PCS and Cyclingnews. Stage 1 favourites: Evenepoel's participation was uncertain on March 20-21 due to being stranded on Tenerife by severe weather, but comments dated March 23 on cyclinguptodate.com suggest the race is underway as normal — however his participation status should be verified. UK platform note: Discovery+ is the streaming home for Stages 1–3; the switch to HBO Max begins Thursday March 26 with Stage 4. US broadcaster is Peacock (ASO rights), not HBO Max — a critical distinction. No 1.1 or above races identified on today's calendar beyond this stage.

issues: Remco Evenepoel's participation in Stage 1 was uncertain as of March 21 due to severe weather stranding him on Tenerife; search results from March 23 contain race-day comments suggesting the race is proceeding, but his actual start has not been independently confirmed in available sources.

UK broadcast platform is in transition: Stages 1–3 stream on Discovery+; Stage 4 onwards moves to HBO Max. This is confirmed by road.cc, Cycling Weekly and Cyclist.co.uk. The stream_url for Stage 1 correctly points to discoveryplus.com/gb.
US broadcast is Peacock (via ASO rights deal through 2029), not HBO Max — the two platforms carry different races and should not be confused.
Stage 1 finish time (~17:00 CET) is estimated; actual finish will depend on race speed and any delays.

No other UCI 1.1 or above races identified on March 23 2026 beyond Volta a Catalunya Stage 1. The next concurrent 1.1+ events begin March 24 (Settimana Internazionale Coppi e Bartali Stage 1, 2.1) and March 25 (Ronde van Brugge men, WT).
